text: 100g of fat. saturated fat, cholesterol, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. the rest is not doing much. not relevant here.
0g of protein. everything else is kind of whatever. also not relevant here.
vitamin e at 11% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
everything else is kind of whatever.

the upsides first: highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

on the weaker side: extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.
A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
